amendments.text: (a) At speeds up to VA, the vertical tail surfaces must be designed to withstand- (1) A sudden displacement of the rudder control (with the airplane in unaccelerated flight with zero yaw) to the maximum deflection allowed by the control stops or by pilot strength, whichever is critical; (2) A yaw angle of 15 degrees with the rudder fully deflected (except as limited by pilot strength) in the direction tending to increase the slip; and (3) A yaw angle of 15 degrees with the rudder control maintained in the neutral position (except as limited by pilot strength). (b) The average loading of B23.11 and figure 1 of Appendix B and the distribution in figures 7, 6, and 8 of Appendix B may be used instead of the requirements of subparagraphs (a)(1), (a)(2), and (a)(3), respectively. (c) The yaw angles specified in paragraph (a)(3) of this section may be reduced if the yaw angle chosen for a particular speed cannot be exceeded in-- (1) Steady slip conditions; (2) Uncoordinated rolls from steep banks; or (3) Sudden failure of the critical engine with delayed corrective action.
